chunk_summary: The checklist for school-based nutrition and micronutrient programs under PM POSHAN ensures meal acceptance, supervised IFA dosing, proper deworming protocols, and fortified staple transitions to address pediatric anemia, growth, and infection risks effectively.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of schools in delivering PM POSHAN meals and preventive health interventions.
Learn to verify adherence to weekly IFA dosing and deworming protocols in schools.
Identify strategies to manage fortified staple transitions and address acceptability concerns.
Develop actionable school-based follow-up plans for children with anemia or other health risks.
key_facts: PM POSHAN provides daily hot cooked meals to schoolchildren as part of India's nutrition programs.
Weekly IFA tablets must be age-appropriate, supervised during intake, and monitored for side effects.
Biannual National Deworming Day excludes acutely unwell children and requires adverse-event protocols.
Fortified rice introduction may face acceptability challenges; micro-dose framing can improve uptake.
School-based checklists prevent 'coverage on paper' from being mistaken for true nutritional intake.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: PM POSHAN provides daily hot cooked meals, but the school platform also acts as a delivery site for preventive interventions that sit under the health system (weekly IFA, biannual NDD, and screening campaigns).
document_key_topics: PM POSHAN meal delivery and monitoring
